content:
First note that any block of s consecutive 1’s has value  
   
   Jₛ = 11…1 (s digits) = (10ˢ−1)/9.  
   
If we use m₁ blocks of size 1, m₂ blocks of size 2 and m₃ blocks of size 3 (no block can be larger, since 1111=9999/9>1000), then the equation  
  
  m₁·9 + m₂·99 + m₃·999 = 9000  
  
(multiplying both sides of ∑Jₛ=1000 by 9) becomes  
   
  m₁ + 11 m₂ +111 m₃ =1000.  
  
Once (m₁,m₂,m₃) is chosen, the total length is  
   
  n = m₁·1 + m₂·2 + m₃·3 = 1000 −9(m₂+12 m₃).  
  
So letting t = m₂ +12 m₃, one sees that 0≤t≤108 but t=107 is impossible (no non‐negative solution), and exactly the other 108 integers 0,1,2,…,106,108 occur.  Hence there are  
   
  a = 108  
   
possible values of n, and the sum of the digits of 108 is  
   
  1+0+8 = 9.  
  
Answer: \boxed{9}.
